title: navarretia matthewsii
intro: Navarretia matthewsii, also known as Matthews' pincushion, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae). It is a herbaceous perennial with small, white or pink flowers, commonly found in the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. Navarretia matthewsii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
